description: Basic numerals and their usage.
Expressions related to money, prices, and currencies.
Ways to express time (hours, minutes, periods).

russian: Expressions of Dates and Days of the Week
description: Names of the days of the week, months, and date format.
Pronouns 这 (this), 那 (that) and their derivatives.
Difference in usage of the two ways to say 'two'.

russian: 几 vs 多少 (question about quantity)
description: Difference between questions 'how many?' for small and large/indefinite quantities.

russian: 不 vs 没 (both mean negation)
description: Difference between negations '不' (general/future) and '没' (fact/past).
Constructions with the copula verb 是 (to be).

chinese: 是 vs 有 vs 在
english: 是(shì) vs 有(yǒu) vs 在(zài)
russian: 是 (to be) vs 有 (to have/to exist) vs 在 (to be located)
description: Difference between the three main verbs of existence and location.
Construction for emphasizing the time, place, or manner of an action in the past.
Difference between the attributive particle 的 and the adverbial particle 地.
Difference in expressing ability (learned vs physical) and possibility.
Words indicating location and direction (上, 下, 里, 外, etc.).
Basic types of questions using question words.
Subtle differences between modal verbs of possibility and permission.
Multifunctional particle indicating a change of situation or completion of an action.

english: The Tense of Past – 了(le) vs 过(guò)
russian: Expressing Past Tense – 了 vs 过
description: Comparison of particles 了 (completed action) and 过 (past experience).

chinese: 一点儿 vs 有点儿
english: 一点儿(yìdiǎnr) vs 有点儿(yǒudiǎnr)
russian: 一点儿 vs 有点儿 (a little/slightly)
description: 一点儿 (a little + noun) vs 有点儿 (slightly + adjective/verb).

chinese: 吗 vs 呢 vs 吧
english: 吗(ma) vs 呢(ne) vs 吧(ba)
russian: Interrogative particles 吗, 呢, 吧
description: Difference between question particles: 吗 (yes/no question), 呢 (contextual question), 吧 (assumption/request).

chinese: 想 vs 要 vs 想要
english: 想(xiǎng) vs 要(yào) vs 想要(xiǎngyào)
russian: 想 vs 要 vs 想要 (to want)
description: Gradations of expressing desire: 想 (to want/think), 要 (to want/need), 想要 (to wish for).

chinese: 正在 vs 正 vs 在
english: The Tense of Ongoing – 正在(zhèngzài) vs 正(zhèng) vs 在(zài)
russian: Present Continuous Tense – 正在 vs 正 vs 在
description: Ways to express an action in progress using 正, 在, 正在.
Adverbs that modify the degree of an attribute (很, 非常, 太, 最, 更, etc.).
The simplest type of question requiring a 'yes/no' answer.
A question offering a choice between options.
Construction for asking again or clarifying information.

english: Questions to ask one’s opinion – 好吗、可以吗、怎么样、行吗
russian: Questions to ask for an opinion – 好吗, 可以吗, 怎么样, 行吗
description: Phrases for polite suggestions or requests for permission.
A question formed by repeating the verb in affirmative and negative form (V不V).

english: The Tense of Future – 要/快/快要/就要……了
russian: Expressing Future Tense – 要/快/快要/就要……了
description: Ways to express near and general future.
The particle 着, indicating a continuous state or background for another action.
Constructions of the type 'there is/find something somewhere' with verbs 有, 是, 在.
Reduplication of verbs to soften the tone, express briefness, or attempt an action.
Reduplication of adjectives to intensify or soften a description.

russian: 从 vs 离 (prepositions of place)
description: 从 (from, out of, since) indicates the point of departure, 离 (from) indicates the distance between points.
Polysemous word: verb 'to give' and preposition 'for/to'.
Constructions with verbs like 'give', 'tell', having a direct and indirect object.
Basic types of compound sentences.
Use of conjunctions/prepositions to link objects and indicate joint action.

russian: 还是 vs 或者 ('or' in questions and statements)
description: 还是 ('or') is used in questions, 或者 ('or') is used in statements.

russian: 应该 vs 该 (should, ought to)
description: Modal verbs of obligation. 该 is more common in spoken language.

chinese: 朝 vs 向 vs 往
english: 朝(cháo) vs 向(xiàng) vs 往(wǎng)
russian: 朝 vs 向 vs 往 (prepositions of direction)
description: Prepositions indicating direction of movement: 朝/向 (facing/towards), 往 (movement towards).
全 (whole, entire) as a determiner and 都 (all) as an adverb expressing completeness.

chinese: 刚刚 vs 刚才 vs 刚
english: 刚刚(gānggāng) vs 刚才(gāngcái) vs 刚(gāng)
russian: 刚刚 vs 刚才 vs 刚 (just, just now)
description: Difference in usage of words denoting the recent past. 刚才 – noun, 刚/刚刚 – adverbs.

russian: 以为 vs 认为 (to think, to believe)
description: 以为 – mistaken opinion, 认为 – personal opinion or judgment.
Basic and most frequent measure words (个, 本, 张, 只, etc.).
Measure words for pairs, groups, categories of objects.
Measure words for abstract concepts, types of activities, etc.
Basic comparison constructions with 比, 没有, 不如.
Comparison constructions indicating the degree of difference (by how much more/less).
Constructions 'as... as...' (跟...一样) and 'not as... as...'.

chinese: 一会儿 vs 一下 vs 一下子
english: 一会儿(yīhuìr) vs 一下(yīxià) vs 一下子(yīxiàzi)
russian: 一会儿 vs 一下 vs 一下子 (a little, quickly)
description: 一会儿 (short time), 一下 (short action/attempt), 一下子 (suddenly/instantly).
Basic verbs and adjectives acting as resultative complements (见, 懂, 完, 好, etc.).
Simple directional complements (来, 去), indicating movement relative to the speaker.
Describing the state of the subject or object after an action using 得.
A complement expressing the quantity of actions (number of times) or duration.

english: Modal Particles for Beginners – 吗/吧/呢/啊/喂/的
description: Basic modal particles expressing a question, assumption, exclamation, confirmation.
More specific and less frequently used measure words.
Measure words used in written and formal styles.
Ways to express approximate quantity (几, 多, 左右, 来).
Sentences where two or more verbs share the same subject, expressing sequence or purpose.
The adverb 还 with meanings 'still', 'also', 'in addition', 'relatively'.
忽然 (adverb, more neutral) vs 突然 (adjective/adverb, stronger).
常常 (general frequency) vs 往往 (regularity under certain conditions).

chinese: 常常 vs 经常 vs 时常
english: 常常(chángcháng) vs 经常(jīngcháng) vs 时常(shícháng)
russian: 常常 vs 经常 vs 时常 (often)
description: Subtle differences in meaning and usage frequency of frequency adverbs.
Reduplication like '一个个' to express 'each one individually' or gradualness.
Verb constructions like '见面', '睡觉', which can be separated by other words.

chinese: 又 vs 还 vs 再
english: 又(yòu) vs 还(hái) vs 再(zài)
russian: 又 vs 还 vs 再 (again, still, yet)
description: 又 (happened again), 还 (continues/is added), 再 (will happen again in the future).

chinese: 一直 vs 总是 vs 老是
english: 一直(yìzhí) vs 总是(zǒngshì) vs 老是(lǎoshì)
russian: 一直 vs 总是 vs 老是 (always, constantly)
description: 一直 (continuously), 总是 (always as a rule), 老是 (always, often with a negative connotation).

chinese: 左右 vs 前后 vs 上下
english: 左右(zuǒyòu) vs 前后(qiánhòu) vs 上下(shàngxià)
russian: 左右 vs 前后 vs 上下 (approximately, about)
description: 左右 (for numbers, age), 前后 (for time, events), 上下 (for levels, ages, less common).

chinese: 大概 vs 大约 vs 恐怕
english: 大概(dàgài) vs 大约(dàyuē) vs 恐怕(kǒngpà)
russian: 大概 vs 大约 vs 恐怕 (probably, approximately)
description: 大概/大约 (probably/approximately), 恐怕 (I'm afraid... / expresses concern).
Common prefixes (e.g., 老-, 小-, 第-) and suffixes (e.g., -子, -儿, -头).
Questions that don't require an answer but express certainty or emotion.
Constructions where the object of the first verb is the subject of the second (请他来).
More complex types of pivotal sentences with different verb groups (使, 让, 叫, 派).
Complex sentences with subordinate clauses of reason, condition, concession.
Basic passive constructions with 被, indicating an action performed on the subject.
Basic 把 construction, indicating an action performed on an object and its change.
Construction 'V + Obj + V + Comp' to describe the result of an action (他唱歌唱得很好).
More complex and idiomatic resultative complements.
Compound directional complements (上来, 下去, 进来, 出去, etc.).
Expressing possibility/impossibility of an action using 得/不 + complement.
Expressing a high degree using 极了, 死了, 得很, 得不得了, etc.
More complex cases of quantitative complements, including comparison.
Complex sentences with subordinate clauses of purpose, consequence, time.

english: 越(yuè) …… 越(yuè) …… / 越来越(yuè lái yuè……)
russian: The more..., the more... / More and more...
description: Constructions for expressing progressive change.

russian: 才 vs 就 (adverbs of time/condition)
description: 才 (late, few, only then) vs 就 (early, many, immediately).

russian: 按照 vs 根据 (according to, based on)
description: 按照 (according to a plan, rule) vs 根据 (based on facts, data).

russian: 跟……一样 / 像……一样 (the same as; similar to)
description: Comparison constructions for similarity and resemblance.

english: 别的(bié de) vs 另外(lìngwài)
russian: 别的 vs 另外 (other, another)
description: 别的 (other, others – as a determiner) vs 另外 (other, additional – often with 还/再).
Large numbers, fractions, percentages, ordinal numerals.

english: The extended uses of interrogative pronouns
description: Using 谁, 什么, 哪儿, etc. to mean 'someone', 'something', 'somewhere' or 'every/any'.
Constructions with two negatives to express an emphatic affirmative (不得不, 没有不).
Rules for the order of multiple attributives before a noun.

russian: 及时 vs 按时 (timely, on time)
description: 及时 (in time, without delay, at the right moment) vs 按时 (on schedule, at the appointed time).

russian: 对于 vs 关于 (regarding, about)
description: 对于 (with regard to, for) vs 关于 (regarding, on the topic of, about).

russian: 经过 vs 通过 (via, by means of)
description: 经过 (to pass through a place/process) vs 通过 (by means of, using).

russian: 本来 vs 原来 (originally, turns out)
description: 本来 (originally, by nature) vs 原来 (formerly; turns out, as it appears).
几乎 (almost, nearly – often with negative outcomes) vs 差不多 (almost, approximately – more colloquial).
An adverb expressing an unexpected turn or contrast, placed after the subject.

chinese: 渐渐 vs 慢慢 vs 逐步 vs 逐渐
pinyin: jiànjiàn vs mànmàn vs zhúbù vs zhújiàn
english: 渐渐(jiànjiàn) vs 慢慢(mànmàn) vs 逐步(zhúbù) vs 逐渐(zhújiàn)
russian: 渐渐 vs 慢慢 vs 逐步 vs 逐渐 (gradually)
description: Nuances of 'gradually': 渐渐/逐渐 (natural process), 慢慢 (slowly), 逐步 (step by step, systematically).
Figurative (abstract) meanings of compound directional complements.
More complex forms of the 把 construction with various types of complements.
Passive constructions without an agent, also with 让, 叫.
More complex constructions with verbs of motion, appearance, and disappearance.
Constructions 'the more..., the more...' (越…越…) and 'compared to...' (比起…来).

chinese: 赶紧 vs 赶快 vs 立刻 vs 连忙
pinyin: gǎnjǐn vs gǎnkuài vs lìkè vs liánmáng
english: 赶紧 (gǎnjǐn) vs 赶快 (gǎnkuài) vs 立刻 (lìkè) vs 连忙 (liánmáng)
russian: 赶紧 vs 赶快 vs 立刻 vs 连忙 (quickly, immediately)
description: 赶紧/赶快 (hastily, without delay), 立刻 (instantly, right away), 连忙 (immediately, in response to something).

chinese: 由于 vs 因为 vs 为了
english: 由于(yóuyú) vs 因为(yīnwèi) vs 为了(wèile)
russian: 由于 vs 因为 vs 为了 (due to, because, for the sake of)
description: 由于 (more formal cause), 因为 (general cause, often paired with 所以), 为了 (purpose).

chinese: 光 vs 仅(仅) vs 就
english: 光 (guāng) vs 仅(仅) (jǐnjǐn) vs 就 (jiù)
russian: 光 vs 仅 vs 就 (only, merely)
description: 光 (only, colloquial, often negative connotation), 仅 (only, formal), 就 (for limitation/emphasis).

chinese: 差(一)点儿 vs 差不多
english: 差（一）点儿 (chà yīdiǎnr) vs 差不多 (chàbùduō)
russian: 差(一)点儿 vs 差不多 (almost but not quite vs approximately)
description: 差(一)点儿 (almost, nearly – about an event that didn't happen) vs 差不多 (almost, approximately).
Detailed breakdown of all meanings and functions of the adverb 就: time, limitation, agreement, consequence.
Detailed breakdown of all meanings and functions of the adverb 才: later time, small degree, condition.

chinese: 由 vs 自从 vs 将
english: 由 (yóu) vs 自从 (zìcóng) vs 将 (jiāng)
russian: 由 vs 自从 vs 将 (prepositions/particles)
description: 由 (by, due to, from), 自从 (since a time), 将 (will, bookish future tense).
Various extended constructions with the verb 有, including comparison and evaluation (有+Adj).

russian: 偶尔 vs 偶然 (occasionally, accidentally)
description: 偶尔 (occasionally, frequency adverb) vs 偶然 (accidental, adjective/adverb).

russian: 竟然 vs 居然 (unexpectedly, contrary to expectations)
description: 竟然 (expresses surprise, often negative) vs 居然 (stronger surprise, can be positive or negative).

chinese: 对……来说 vs 拿……来说
pinyin: duì… lái shuō vs ná… lái shuō
english: 对……来说 vs 拿……来说
russian: 对……来说 vs 拿……来说 (for someone; take for example)
description: 对……来说 (from someone's point of view) vs 拿……来说 (take as an example).

chinese: 到底 vs 究竟 vs 终于 vs 毕竟
pinyin: dàodǐ vs jiūjìng vs zhōngyú vs bìjìng
english: 到底 (dàodǐ) vs 究竟 (jiūjìng) vs 终于 (zhōngyú) vs 毕竟 (bìjìng)
russian: 到底 vs 究竟 vs 终于 vs 毕竟 (in the end, after all)
description: 到底/究竟 (questions to get to the bottom of something), 终于 (finally, result), 毕竟 (after all, in the final analysis – justification/explanation).
Measure words in idiomatic and fixed expressions.

chinese: 从而 vs 一旦 vs 完了 vs 加上
pinyin: cóng'ér vs yídàn vs wánle vs jiāshàng
english: 从而 (cónɡ’ér) vs 一旦 (yídàn) vs 完了 (wánle) vs 加上 (jiāshànɡ)
russian: 从而 vs 一旦 vs 完了 vs 加上 (conjunctions/insertions)
description: 从而 (thus, formally), 一旦 (if once, whenever), 完了 (colloquial: then, afterwards), 加上 (plus, besides).
Short, idiomatic forms of complex sentences (一…就…, 越…越…, 不…不…).
Advanced constructions: 'the more..., the more...' (愈…愈…), 'with each passing day increasingly' (日见…).
把 construction combined with modal verbs, negation, and other complex structures.
Complex passive constructions, including 被...给..., 被...所..., and other variants.
Super-complex cases of directional complements with three syllables and their figurative meanings.
Complex and idiomatic possibilities/impossibilities, as well as their negation.
Literary and emotionally charged expressions of high degree (之至, 不堪, 万分).
Complex and extended descriptions of state with phrases and clauses after 得.
Multi-layered complex sentences with several subordinate clauses.
Complex cases, including constructions with evaluative adjectives (嫌, 讨厌), etc.

russian: 偏偏 vs 偏 (insistently, contrary to expectations)
description: 偏偏 (stubbornly, despite everything, unfortunately) vs 偏 (shorter form, often with 要, 不).
Archaic, dialectal, or highly specialized measure words.
Less productive and bookish affixes, their stylistic features.
Stylistic and pragmatic features of the use of directional complements.
Stylistic restrictions, cases of obligatory and prohibited use of the 把 construction.
Use of complex sentences in written language and journalism.
Passive constructions in news, official documents, and literary language.
Modal particle expressing obviousness, expectation, gentle urging, or marking a topic.

english: 罢了 (bà le) vs 而已 (éryǐ)
russian: 罢了 vs 而已 (nothing but, that's all)
description: 罢了 (colloquial, often with 不过/无非, downplaying) vs 而已 (more formal/written, with 仅仅/只).
Measure words in poetry, classical literature, and their historical development.
Historical development of the passive and its analogues in Classical Chinese.
Comparative constructions in Classical Chinese and their modern equivalents.
本 (this, our, the said – formal style) vs 此 (this, the said – literary/formal style).

chinese: 刚好 vs 恰好 vs 正好
english: 刚好(gānghǎo) vs 恰好(qiàhǎo) vs 正好(zhènghǎo)
russian: 刚好 vs 恰好 vs 正好 (just, exactly, right)
description: 刚好 (coincidental match), 恰好 (precise correspondence), 正好 (both; the most suitable).
Adverb meaning 'all', 'nothing but', 'completely' (often with a negative connotation).
特 (colloquial 'very') vs 异常 (extremely, abnormally – written).

russian: 一齐 vs 一同 (together, simultaneously)
description: 一齐 (simultaneously, synchronously) vs 一同 (together, jointly).
An adverb predicting the inevitable occurrence of an event in the future.

chinese: 不时 vs 一时 vs 时而
pinyin: bù shí vs yī shí vs shí'ér
english: 不时(bù shí) vs 一时(yī shí) vs 时而(shí’ér)
russian: 不时 vs 一时 vs 时而 (from time to time, temporarily)
description: 不时 (from time to time), 一时 (for a while, temporarily; in combinations 一时+not), 时而 (sometimes..., sometimes...).
Emotionally charged particle/adverb for emphasis, contrast, surprise, questions.

chinese: 时常 vs 时刻 vs 时时
english: 时常(shícháng) vs 时刻(shíkè) vs 时时(shíshí)
russian: 时常 vs 时刻 vs 时时 (often, constantly)
description: 时常 (often), 时刻 (every moment, constantly – with 准备着, 牢记), 时时 (constantly, from time to time – more literary).
A preposition introducing a condition or process that causes a change.
A bookish particle forming passive constructions (被...所...), nominal phrases (所+V), etc.
A formal variant of 'with/and' (跟/和), often used in official names and documents.
A literary and formal conjunction 'and' and preposition 'with'. Often found in written language and names.
